---
title: "cancer type"
author: "MJY"
date: "2025-09-08"
output: html_document
---

################################################################################ 

# 

# OUTLINES:

# Use ggplot2 to visualize and compare ranking differences between male and female across cancer types.

# 

################################################################################ 

```{r}
# Load required libraries
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggplot2))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyr))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(forcats))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggtext))

# Create input data frame (cancer types with male and female ranks)
df <- data.frame(
  Cancer = c("LIHC","LUSC","SARC","KICH","HNSC","THCA","PCPG","BLCA",
             "LUAD","CHOL","ESCA","KIRC","PAAD","READ","BRCA","KIRP",
             "COAD","STAD"),
  Male   = c(2,4,6,3,8,8,8,10,6,4,15,10,9,14,16,18,19,21),
  Female = c(1,4,3,7,3,3,4,4,12,15,8,18,20,17,19,20,23,21)
)

# Convert data to long format
df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Male, Female),
               names_to = "Sex",
               values_to = "Rank")

# Calculate the best (lowest) rank for each cancer type
df_min <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Cancer) %>%
  summarise(best_rank = min(Rank))

# Identify the top-ranked cancer type overall
top_cancer <- df_min %>%
  slice_min(best_rank, n = 1) %>%
  pull(Cancer)

# Reorder cancer types based on best rank (higher rank displayed on top)
df_long <- df_long %>%
  left_join(df_min, by = "Cancer") %>%
  mutate(Cancer = fct_reorder(Cancer, best_rank, .desc = TRUE))

# Customize y-axis labels (highlight top cancer in red and bold)
y_labels <- levels(df_long$Cancer)

# Create plot
p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Cancer, y = Rank, color = Sex)) +
  geom_point(size = 6, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(group = Cancer), color = "grey10", linewidth = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Male" = "#1f78b4", "Female" = "#e31a1c")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Male vs Female Rank in Cancer Types",
       y = "Rank", x = "Cancer Type") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = y_labels) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(),  # Enable HTML-style labels
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey70", linetype = "solid"),  # Major grid lines
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey70", linetype = "solid")   # Minor grid lines
  )

p

# Save the plot as a high-resolution image
# ggsave(
#   filename = "F:/WJlab/HCC/TAD_and_Rank/RANK_ggplot.png",
#   plot = p,
#   width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 1000
# )


```
